sanskrit: हन्त ते कथयिष्यामि दिव्या ह्यात्मविभूतयः। प्राधान्यतः कुरुश्रेष्ठ नास्त्यन्तो विस्तरस्य मे॥
transliteration: śrī-bhagavān uvāca / hanta te kathayiṣyāmi divyā hy ātma-vibhūtayaḥ / prādhānyataḥ kuru-śreṣṭha nāsty-anto vistarasya me
literal_meaning: The Blessed Lord said: ‘Yes, I shall speak to you of My divine self-manifestations, O best of the Kurus, but only the principal ones, for there is no end to My expanse.’
contextual_meaning: Krishna signals willingness to share a curated sample of his infinite identity, acknowledging human cognitive limits while inviting sustained curiosity rather than closure.
psychological_state: A secure-attachment moment: the mind is both soothed by the promise of revelation and gently reminded that full knowledge is impossible, converting potential overwhelm into regulated wonder.
trigger_scenario: A respected mentor tells you, ‘I’ll show you a glimpse of how vast this field really is—don’t try to master it all, just start here,’ calming your imposter syndrome while keeping motivation alive.
anchor_text: Infinity can only be hinted; savor the glimpse and keep seeking.
